mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.3pp, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that drives corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during periods of industrial production contraction, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the post-shock recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in a piecewise regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative or zero mean 60-day forward return, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic output that improves corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates that higher borrowing costs are successfully cooling economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances within the tightening regime, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below 50%.
